messages.content:
You are an agent answering a question using these tools: google_search, get_stock_data, compute, get_weather.
At each step, EITHER issue exactly one more natural-language sub-query to gather missing information, OR give the final answer if you already have enough.
Respond with EXACTLY ONE of the following and nothing else:
  <sub-query>a single self-contained, single-tool question</sub-query>
  <final_answer>your concise final answer</final_answer>

Original question: A random variable X has probability density function proportional to cos(x)/sqrt(x) from 1 to 2. What is the normalization constant should this be multiplied by to make this a valid PDF? Round your answer to the nearest integer.

History so far:
Step 1 sub-query: Compute the integral of cos(x)/sqrt(x) from x=1 to x=2
  Tool: compute
  Result: {"query": "integrate cos(x)/sqrt(x) from 1 to 2", "result": "integral_1^2 cos(x)/sqrt(x) dx = 1/2 (E_(1/2)(-i) + E_(1/2)(i) - sqrt(2) (E_(1/2)(-2 i) + E_(1/2)(2 i)))\u22480.0792006", "details": {"Definite integral": "integral_1^2 cos(x)/sqrt(x) dx = 1/2 (E_(1/2)(-i) + E_(1/2)(i) - sqrt(2) (E_(1/2)(-2 i) + E_(1/2)(2 i)))\u22480.0792006", "Visual representation of the integral": null, "Indefinite integral": "integral cos(x)/sqrt(x) dx = -(i (sqrt(-i x) \u0393(1/2, -i x) - sqrt(i x) \u0393(1/2, i x)))/(2 sqrt(x)) + constant"}}

Your next action:

response:
<final_answer>
The normalization constant is the reciprocal of the integral. Since the integral of cos(x)/√x from 1 to 2 equals approximately 0.0792006, the normalization constant is:

1 / 0.0792006 ≈ 12.63

Rounded to the nearest integer: **13**
</final_answer>
